---
title: "Evidence based Training"
author: "Peter Raidl"
date: "2024-10-01"
categories: [blog, science, training, exercise, concept]
draft: True
image: "Training.png"
---

```{=html}
<!--# Outline 
What do I mean with Training?
How did we find out about training and the improvements that are possible?
What is scientific evidence?
What is evidence based decision making?
How can we blend knowledge from practitioner, trainee and science?

-->
```

In the following I want to give a brief introduction to **evidence based training**. This term is already widely used in sport and exercise science but originates from medicine and was popularised mostly by David Sackett [-@sackettEvidenceBasedMedicine1996] and can be viewed as a shift in perspective of how modern medicine should make decisions. The article will start with an introduction to what I mean when I talk about (sports and physical exercise related) training ([@sec-training]). You can easily skip this part if you are familiar with discussions on that topic or you are not interested in terminology and already "know" what *training* means. In [@sec-knowledge] I want to discuss our knowledge base in training before I go into [@sec-scientificmethod] "THE Scientific Method" - a term that started bothering me a little. This part will discuss how we can use scientific evidence (the stuff that you find in research papers) to guide your decisions in training. After that (@sec-ebt) we look at Sackett's ideas of EBM and apply it to training. Finally, I want to discuss how we can blend knowledge from the practitioner, the trainee and science to make the best decisions in training ([@sec-blending]).

As always, this is a blog and represents my personal take on the story. Make up your own mind and keep it with the original founder of Evidence Based Training:

> *"Take what is useful, reject what is useless and add what is specifically yours"* - *Bruce Lee*

# Research based training {#sec-scientificmethod}

# Evidence based decision making {#sec-ebt}

# Bring it together {#sec-blending}
